data.title: January Fed rate cut bets dip after latest labor report
data.description:
Traders revised their rate cut bets for January after the latest labor report came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

After the latest job report, the chances of a 25 basis point rate cut stand at 3.9%, as compared to 11.1% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

U.S. nonfarm payrolls grew by 50K in December, basically in line with the +55K consensus and slightly slower than the +56K in November (revised from +64K), according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

The next Federal Reserve policy decision is slated to be announced on January 28. 

In the last quarter of 2025, the Fed implemented three consecutive rate reductions, bringing down the central bank's policy rate by 75 basis points to a 3.50%-3.75% target range. But Fed officials expect that pace to slow markedly this year, with the median projection calling for just one quarter-point cut.

Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran's forecast calls for 1.5 percentage points of cuts to the federal funds rate target range, he said in a broadcast interview on Thursday. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Stocks to watch on Friday: TSM, GM, BABA, JNJ
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as the key jobs report came largely as expected. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

TSMC (TSM) rose 0.5% in premarket trading after reporting December 2025 revenue of approximately NT$335 billion on a consolidated basis. The figure represents a 2.5% decline from November but a 20.4% increase compared to the same month a year earlier. The chipmaker serves as a key supplier to major technology companies, including Apple, Nvidia, and AMD.

General Motors (GM) fell 2.4% before the opening bell after announcing it will take an additional $7.1 billion charge tied to its decision to scale back electric vehicle production and Chinese operations. The charge comes on top of the $1.6 billion the automaker previously recorded in the three months ended September 30.

Alibaba (BABA) slipped 2.51% in premarket trade despite its Hong Kong-listed shares rallying as much as 3.1%—marking their strongest intraday performance since late 2024. The surge was triggered by a Bloomberg report stating that Beijing is preparing to approve the import of Nvidia’s H200 AI chips for commercial use as early as this quarter.

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) edged down 0.3% in premarket trading after entering an agreement with the Trump administration to reduce drug prices for millions of U.S. patients. The deal exempts the company’s products from U.S. tariffs, while Johnson & Johnson plans to build two new U.S. facilities in North Carolina and Pennsylvania as part of its $55 billion domestic investment in manufacturing and innovation.

data.title: Evolus sees Q4 net revenue between $88.6M and $90.6M
data.description:
• Evolus (EOLS) on Friday posted preliminary unaudited net revenue of $88.6M to $90.6M for Q4, representing 12% to 15% growth over the prior year.
  • The company reaffirmed positive Non-GAAP operating income of $5M to $7M for Q4 2025.
  • Evolus expects net revenue of $295.5M to $297.5M for the FY25, representing 11% to 12% growth over the prior year.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $53 million compared to $43.5 million on September 30, 2025.
  • Consensus for Q4 and FY25 revenues are $90.91M and $297.47M.
  • Total net revenues for 2026 are projected to be between $327 million and $337 million, which represents 11% to 13% growth from preliminary unaudited 2025 results.
  • Evolysse and Estyme injectable HA gels are anticipated to contribute 10% to 12% of total revenue for the full-year 2026, it said.
  • The company expects to achieve sustainable annual profitability1 beginning in 2026.
  • For 2028, net revenue is expected between $450 million and $500 million, representing a three-year CAGR of 15% to 19%, and non-GAAP operating income margins of 13% to 15% for 2028.
  • EOLS +3.65% premarket to $5.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aptevo Therapeutics jumps after securing $60 million equity line of credit
data.description:
• Aptevo Therapeutics (APVO) has entered into a $60 million equity line of credit agreement with Yorkville Advisors Global.
  • The facility provides Aptevo with access to capital, allowing the company to raise funds incrementally, at its discretion, and under market-based conditions. Proceeds from the financing will be used to support ongoing clinical development, advance preclinical programs, and fund general corporate purposes.
  • APVO +9.02% premarket to $10.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bitmine Immersion appoints Young Kim as CFO and COO
data.description:
Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) on Friday announced the appointment of Young Kim as CFO and COO, effective immediately.

In addition, Kim has been appointed a board member. Kim will report to Board Chairman Tom Lee.

From 2021 to 2025, Kim served as a partner and senior portfolio manager at Axiom Investors.

Bitmine is set to hold its annual meeting at the Wynn Las Vegas on January 15, 2026.

Shares were +0.03% pre-market to $31.29.

In November, BitMine Immersion Technologies had announced the appointment of Chi Tsang as CEO and a board member.

data.title: Bonk board member Mitchell Rudy acquires stock to close Q4
data.description:
• Bonk (BNKK) on Friday said Mitchell Rudy, a member of the company's Board of Directors, executed an open-market purchase of 13,142 shares of the company's common stock at the close of the fourth quarter.
  • BNKK +7.22% premarket to $2.97.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scienjoy receives additional 180 days from Nasdaq to meet compliance rules
data.description:
• Scienjoy Holding (NASDAQ:SJ) stated on Friday that Nasdaq granted an additional 180-day extension on Jan. 7, 2026, to regain compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • The company could face delisting if it fails to regain compliance by July 6, 2026, and may seek a review of any delisting determination by a Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • Shares -4.93%.

data.title: Housing starts fall more than expected in September
data.description: Housing starts slid 4.6% M/M to a 1.246M annual rate in September, compared with the 1.330M consensus and the prior month's 1.307M, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday. This was before the government shutdown started Oct. 1.

data.title: flyExclusive prices of $15 million public offering
data.description:
• flyExclusive (FLYX) on Friday announced the pricing of its public offering of 2.26M shares of its common stock at a public offering price of $6.65 per share of common stock.
  • Lucid Capital Markets is acting as the sole book-running manager for the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 12, 202.
  • FLYX -4.15% premarket to $6.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Delcath Systems expects FY revenue of $85.2 million
data.description:
• Delcath Systems (DCTH) said on Friday its total fourth-quarter revenue is expected to be approximately $20.7M vs. a consensus of $19.99M and full-year revenue of $85.2M vs. a consensus of $84.49M, respectively
  • HEPZATO KIT fourth quarter and full-year revenue expected to be approximately $19.0 million and $78.8 million, respectively
  • CHEMOSAT  fourth quarter and full-year revenue expected to be approximately $1.7 million and $6.4 million, respectively
  • The company said it repurchased 628,572 common shares for $6.0 million through December 31, 2025, under the approved $25.0 million Share Buyback Program.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had approximately $91.0 million of cash and short-term investments and no debt compared to cash and short-term investments of $88.9 million as of September 30, 2025
  • DCTH -0.97% premarket to $10.11.
  • Source: Press Release
